_index.title: Color Primitives

_meta.do_not_edit: Edit the source MD; CI regenerates this file.

_index.body: All color palettes follow a shared OKLCH shade formula. Each palette has a named base (500), and all other shades are derived from it. Figma stores these as hex; engineering implements them as OKLCH with relative color syntax.

oklch-shade-formula.title: OKLCH Shade Formula

_meta.do_not_edit: Edit the source MD; CI regenerates this file.

oklch-shade-formula.body:
Engineering derives all shades from the 500 base using the following formula:

> Note on Figma vs Eng: Figma stores colors as hex values. Engineering uses OKLCH with the formula above. There can be minor visual differences between platforms — always defer to the Figma file for design decisions and engineering code for production output.

_index.title: Primitives

_index.body: All color primitives across the system. Every palette follows the same OKLCH shade formula (25–900). Semantic roles are defined in the Semantic Tokens section below.

motion.title: Motion

_meta.do_not_edit: Edit the source MD; CI regenerates this file.

motion.description: Motion tokens cover three dimensions: duration (how long), easing (how it accelerates), and delay (when it starts). All three must be specified together for any animated component.

duration.description: Duration controls the speed of a transition. Smaller, simpler components move faster; larger, complex surfaces take slightly more time to respect their physical weight.

rows.Usage: Micro-feedback: button hovers, checkbox toggles, radio buttons, focus rings
Small scale: tooltip fade-ins, dropdown/select expansions, tag dismissals
Structural changes: collapse/accordion expanding, toast notifications sliding in
Large surfaces: modal scaling in, drawer (side panel) sliding in

easing.description: Easing curves give motion a natural, physical feel.

rows.Usage: Fast start, slow finish. Objects entering the screen (e.g. modals, drawers). Feels responsive but settles smoothly.
Slow start, fast finish. Objects leaving the screen (e.g. closing a side nav, dismissing a toast). Gets out of the user's way quickly.
Smooth start and finish. Elements moving from point A to B, or expanding internally (e.g. accordions opening, progress bars filling).

delay.description: Delay dictates the pause before the motion duration begins.

rows.Usage: Choreography: applied incrementally to list items, table rows, or search result cards as they enter the screen
Hover protection: standard wait time before revealing a tooltip or complex glossary popover. Prevents accidental visual noise.
Feedback holding: used for temporary success states before they auto-dismiss (e.g. a toast lingering before sliding out)

phases.Behavior: Height animates open; content fades in (0→100% opacity) during final 150ms
Height animates closed; content fades out (100→0% opacity) during initial 100ms
Settle time after entry completes before auto-dismiss timer begins

anchor-motion.name: The "Anchor" Motion — Dropdowns, Popovers, and Tooltips

phases.Behavior: Fades in (0→100% opacity) over full duration; element scales up from its trigger point while fading in
Fades out (100→0% opacity) over full duration; element scales down and retracts back to its trigger point

logic_and_accessibility: Intentionality: Apply --zen-motion-delay-intent (300ms) for Tooltip opening on hover to prevent accidental triggers
Immediate Focus: Keyboard focus (Tab) ignores delays and triggers the open motion immediately
Reduced Motion: When prefers-reduced-motion: reduce is detected, fade transitions are disabled and elements toggle visibility instantly

phases.Behavior: Modal scales 95%→100% while background scrim fades in. The slow duration communicates that the user has entered a new, temporary top-level context.
Modal scales 100%→95% and fades 100%→0%. Scrim fades 100%→0%, synchronized. Slower on open to help users track context; dismisses quickly once the decision is made.
A continuous, subtle gradient shimmer moving left-to-right. Not tokenized — value is fixed. Provides visual confirmation the system is active and reduces perceived wait time for data-heavy views.

staggered-entrance.name: Staggered Entrance — Lists, Table Rows, Search Cards
notes: The cascading effect guides the eye naturally downward and prevents the screen feeling "flashed" with too much at once.

phases.Behavior: Subtle background color shift via brightness filter
No motion tokens — instant high-contrast ring/border for accessibility and speed
Subtle background color shift and side stroke draw-in

color-usage-rules.title: Color Usage Rules

_meta.do_not_edit: Edit the source MD; CI regenerates this file.

color-usage-rules.body:
Semantic over primitive. Always use a semantic token (e.g. --zen-color-text-default) rather than a raw palette token (e.g. --zen-color-grey-900) unless you're building a new token. This ensures theming works correctly.

Contrast. Text on backgrounds must meet WCAG AA minimum (4.5:1 for body text, 3:1 for large text). Use the following pairings as reference:

Semantic color intent. Color conveys meaning — don't use success-* colors for non-success contexts, even if the green looks right.

items: text-default on bg-default ✅
text-secondary on bg-subtle ✅
text-disabled should never be used on interactive elements that require action

_meta.do_not_edit: Edit the source MD; CI regenerates this file.

typeface.title: Typeface

_meta.do_not_edit: Edit the source MD; CI regenerates this file.

typeface.body: Use --zen-font-family-text (Roboto) as the default for all UI text. Use --zen-font-family-mono (Roboto Mono) for code snippets, data tables with identifiers, and technical strings. Roobert is the brand typeface only for marketing and communication materials like website, PPTs, and flyers.

minimum-size.body: Must not use type sizes smaller than 11px. You may use 11px text in labels, tags, data visualizations, and supporting text with icon.

accessibility.title: Accessibility

accessibility.body: Don't create one-off font sizes. If your design needs a size between tokens, first check whether a token can be used, then raise it as a proposed token addition.

items: Default body text size is 14px
Minimum body text size is 12px
11px is only acceptable for non-essential UI
Use defined type styles (avoid light weight)
Maintain readable line height and spacing
Text line length is 544px at max with 16px text
Text line length is 480px at max with 14px text

Avoid placeholder-only labels
Avoid long blocks of dense text

spacing-rules.title: Spacing Rules

_meta.do_not_edit: Edit the source MD; CI regenerates this file.

spacing-rules.body:
Use spacing tokens for all margin, padding, and gap values. The scale is:

Never use arbitrary pixel values for spacing. If a layout needs something outside the scale, it's a signal to propose a new token.

items: 2xs–xs for internal element spacing (icon-to-label, checkbox-to-text)
sm–md for internal component padding
lg–xl for spacing between sections and groups
2xl–3xl for page-level layout breathing room

elevation-rules.title: Elevation Rules

_meta.do_not_edit: Edit the source MD; CI regenerates this file.

elevation-rules.body:
Elevation communicates depth and layer hierarchy — not decoration. Only use drop shadows to convey that an element is above another. Never stack multiple shadows, and don't apply elevation to elements sitting on the same visual plane.

Common layer levels: xs → sm → md → lg → xl (each level suggests greater visual separation from the base surface).

_meta.do_not_edit: Edit the source MD; CI regenerates this file.

types-of-states.title: Types of States

_meta.do_not_edit: Edit the source MD; CI regenerates this file.

types-of-states.body:
Interaction States

*Hover states should be suppressed on touch devices to avoid "sticky" visual effects. Conversely, Focus and Pressed states must be supported across all input types to ensure accessibility and tactile feedback.

Feedback & System States

items: Default: The neutral state of an element before any user interaction.
Hover*: Triggered when a cursor is placed over an interactive element.
Focus*: Triggered when an element is highlighted via keyboard, voice, or other input methods.
Pressed*: The momentary state during a physical click or tap.
Dragged: Active when a user presses and moves an element from its original position.
Selected: A persistent state indicating an element has been chosen (e.g. via checkbox, tab, or radio button).
Disabled: An inoperable state where the element cannot be interacted with or focused.
Loading: Indicates a component is processing an action or fetching data.
Error: Indicates invalid input or a failed system action, usually via color or icons.
Success: Indicates valid input or a successfully completed action.
Warning: Indicates a non-blocking issue or a state requiring user caution.
Read-Only: Content is legible and focusable for copying, but cannot be edited.
Indeterminate: A "partial" state, typically for parent checkboxes with mixed child selections.

implementation.title: Implementation

_meta.do_not_edit: Edit the source MD; CI regenerates this file.

implementation.body:
Interactive color states (hover, pressed/active, disabled) are not handled with tokens. Engineering implements these using CSS brightness filters at the component level.

The only tokenized interactive state is focus — see --zen-focus-ring-primary and --zen-focus-ring-error in Section 2.5.

_index.title: Breakpoints

_meta.do_not_edit: Edit the source MD; CI regenerates this file.

_index.body: The majority of Actian users are on desktop. Desktop (lg) is the primary design target. However, pages should be responsive and tested across the full breakpoint range — from xl (large screens) down to sm (phone landscape) — to ensure usability across contexts.

columns-gutters-and-margins.title: Columns, Gutters, and Margins

_meta.do_not_edit: Edit the source MD; CI regenerates this file.

columns-gutters-and-margins.body: Layouts are built on a column grid with three key measurements:

items: Columns — guide what the content aligns to
Gutters — fixed spacing between columns
Margins — negative space beyond the content area

grid-settings.title: Grid Settings

_meta.do_not_edit: Edit the source MD; CI regenerates this file.

grid-settings.body: Target XL and until S. XS is not considered at this moment.

focus-ring-rules.title: Focus Ring Rules

_meta.do_not_edit: Edit the source MD; CI regenerates this file.

focus-ring-rules.body:
Applies to: Buttons, links, checkboxes, radios, avatars, breadcrumbs, tags, toggles, tabs

Use --zen-focus-ring-primary with --zen-focus-ring-offset (2px) as an outline-offset.

Applies to: Inputs, textareas

Use --zen-focus-ring-primary as outline with no offset. The border of the input itself acts as the boundary.

Applies to: Destructive actions and error-state interactive elements

Use --zen-focus-ring-error instead of primary.

Do not use box-shadow to simulate focus rings — it won't work correctly in high-contrast mode.

border-usage.title: Border Usage

_meta.do_not_edit: Edit the source MD; CI regenerates this file.

border-usage.body: Use --zen-border-default for standard container borders (cards, panels, inputs at rest). Use --zen-border-subtle for dividers and separators between rows or sections. Use --zen-border-disabled for any input, button, or form element in a disabled state.

placeholder-text.title: Placeholder Text

_meta.do_not_edit: Edit the source MD; CI regenerates this file.

placeholder-text.body: Two tiers of placeholder exist for inputs: --zen-color-text-placeholder is the standard placeholder (grey-600). --zen-color-text-placeholder-subtle (grey-400) is used in search fields where less visual weight is appropriate.
